title: 10 Herbal Teas For Infection
intro_desc: Herbal teas have been used for centuries to help fight off infections. Herbs like Echinacea purpurea and Echinacea angustifolia are well-known for their immune-boosting properties. These plants contain compounds that help stimulate the immune system, which is the body's natural defense against infections. When we drink tea made from these herbs, these compounds are released into our system, where they can start to work their magic. For example, Echinacea purpurea has been shown to increase the production of white blood cells, which are the cells that help fight off infections.  Another herb that's great for infections is Eucalyptus globulus. This plant has natural anti-inflammatory properties, which means it can help reduce swelling and pain associated with infections. When we drink tea made from Eucalyptus globulus, the anti-inflammatory compounds are released into our system, where they can help soothe and calm the body. This can be especially helpful when we're feeling under the weather and need some relief.  Drinking herbal teas for infections can bring a number of benefits to our lives. For one, they can help us recover from illnesses more quickly. By stimulating our immune system and reducing inflammation, these teas can help our bodies heal faster. This means we can get back to our normal routine sooner, which is always a good thing. Additionally, herbal teas can be a natural and gentle way to manage symptoms, without the risk of side effects that can come with medication. This makes them a great option for people who want to try a more holistic approach to health.
faq_1: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent infections from forming. Some herbal teas, like echinacea and sage, have properties that can boost the immune system. When we're feeling stressed or run down, these teas can help our bodies fight off germs and illness. They might also reduce inflammation, which can make it harder for infections to take hold.
faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent infection from forming by boosting the immune system. Some herbal teas, like echinacea, contain antioxidants that fight off germs and bacteria. These teas can also help reduce inflammation, which can make it harder for infections to take hold. This can help keep you healthy and feeling your best.
faq_safe: While herbal teas can be a great way to soothe a sore throat or calm a cough, consuming them daily for infection can be a concern. Some herbal teas may interact with medications or worsen underlying health conditions. Moderation is key, so consider having a cup or two a day, but not as a replacement for medical treatment.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with infections, but it depends on the type and severity. For mild issues like a sore throat, results can be seen in a few days. For more serious infections like a urinary tract infection, it may take a week or two to feel the effects.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for infection is best in the morning or early afternoon. This allows the body to absorb the beneficial compounds before symptoms worsen. Many herbal teas take effect quickly, so timing your drink to coincide with meals can enhance their impact.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for infection. Some herbs like St. John's Wort can speed up the breakdown of certain antibiotics, making them less effective. Other herbs like goldenseal can slow down the breakdown of some medications, leading to higher levels of the medication in your body. This can cause side effects or reduce the medication's effectiveness.
faq_pregnant: Herbal teas can be a tempting remedy for infections during pregnancy, but it's essential to be cautious. Some herbs, like pennyroyal and blue cohosh, can cause problems for you and your baby. It's best to stick with well-known, safe herbs like peppermint or chamomile, and always check the label for any warnings or concerns.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for infection can be a good choice while breastfeeding. Many herbal teas are considered safe, like peppermint and chamomile. However, some herbal teas contain ingredients that can pass into breast milk and affect the baby. Always check the ingredients and choose teas that are known to be safe for breastfeeding mothers.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas contains bioactive constituents such as alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and polyphenols. These compounds work together to boost the body's immune system, helping to fight off infections. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea tea have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and pain associated with infections. The caffeic acid derivatives in this tea have antimicrobial properties, directly targeting and killing bacteria and other pathogens that cause infections. By consuming Echinacea purpurea tea, the body's natural defenses are enhanced, allowing it to recover from infections more effectively.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for infection
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea root and flowers.
Combine the Echinacea mixture with 1 cup of boiling water in a large mug.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it.
Add honey to taste, if desired, and stir well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for up to 7 days to help fight infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia teas contains several bioactive constituents, including alkylamides, glycoproteins, and polyphenols. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and fight off infections. The polyphenols in Echinacea angustifolia tea have antioxidant properties, which protect the body from free radicals that can damage cells and contribute to infection. The glycoproteins, specifically the polysaccharides, stimulate the immune system to produce more white blood cells, which are essential for fighting off infection. This combination of anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties makes Echinacea angustifolia tea a valuable natural remedy for infections.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea angustifolia herbal teas for infection
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea angustifolia root.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the dried root to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help fight infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas contains several bioactive constituents, including eucalyptol, a compound that has antimicrobial properties. Eucalyptol helps to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, which can cause infections. The tea also contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and promote healing. Additionally, the tea contains tannins, which have antiseptic properties that can help to prevent the spread of infection. By combining these properties, eucalyptus globulus tea can help to combat infections and promote a healthy recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for infection
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Eucalyptus globulus leaves. You can use dried leaves as a substitute.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add 1 tablespoon of Eucalyptus globulus leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day to help with infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra teas contains active constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides. These compounds have antimicrobial properties that help fight off infections. The flavonoids, specifically quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory effects that reduce swelling and ease symptoms. The phenolic acids, including ferulic and sinapic acid, have antioxidant properties that neutralize free radicals and promote healing. The glycosides, including sambunigrin and sambuine, have immunomodulatory effects that boost the body's natural defenses against infection.
remedies.image_alt: Sambucus nigra herbal teas for infection
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Sambucus nigra flowers or 1 teaspoon of dried flowers.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Pour the boiling water over the flowers in a cup.
Steep for 5-10 minutes. Strain the liquid into another cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help with infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas contains active constituents like gingerols and shogaols, which have antibacterial and antifungal properties. These compounds help to inhibit the growth of microorganisms, thereby preventing infections from spreading. The antimicrobial properties of Zingiber officinale teas also help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the body. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties are attributed to the presence of gingerols and shogaols, which can help to reduce swelling and ease pain. By consuming Zingiber officinale teas, the body's natural defenses are supported, allowing it to fight off infections more effectively.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for infection
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Zingiber officinale (ginger root) and a cup of boiling water.
Measure out 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the dried ginger root in a cup or teapot.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the ginger root to release its flavor and medicinal properties.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the ginger root bits.
Drink the tea while it's warm, 2-3 times a day, to help alleviate infection symptoms.

remedy_amazon.title: FGO Organic Ginger Tea, 100 Count, Eco-Conscious Tea Bags, Caffeine Free, Packaging May Vary (Pack of 1)

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are bioactive constituents that help fight infections. Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have strong antioxidant properties that protect the body from damage caused by free radicals. Terpenoids, including ginkgolides and bilobalide, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off infection. The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of Ginkgo biloba help boost the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off infections. By reducing inflammation and protecting the body from damage, Ginkgo biloba teas can help alleviate symptoms of infections and support the healing process.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal teas for infection
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Ginkgo biloba leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Combine the leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the active ingredients to release.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce the risk of infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas contains licorice extract, which is rich in bioactive compounds like glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and ease pain caused by infections. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in the tea have antioxidant properties, which help fight off free radicals and prevent oxidative stress. The antimicrobial properties of the tea's bioactive compounds inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses, helping to clear up infections. By reducing inflammation and fighting off pathogens, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can help alleviate symptoms and support the body's natural healing process.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for infection
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots, 1 quart of water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried roots per 1 cup of water. Add roots to the tea infuser or strainer.
Heat the water in a pot until it reaches 212°F (100°C). Pour the hot water over the roots in the tea infuser.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup using the tea infuser or strainer.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help with infection. Store any leftover tea in the fridge for up to 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas contains thymol and carvacrol, two powerful compounds that fight infection. Thymol acts as an antimicrobial agent, killing bacteria and fungi that cause illness. Carvacrol has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and discomfort in infected areas. These compounds also have antioxidant properties, protecting the body from damage caused by free radicals. Thymus vulgaris teas may be effective in treating respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, due to its ability to soothe and calm the airways.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal teas for infection
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers, or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it boils.
Add the Thymus vulgaris leaves or flowers to the boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 3-4 times a day, to help fight infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum teas contains bioactive compounds like silymarin, silibinin, and silidianin. These compounds have strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to fight off infections. Silymarin, in particular, has been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, enhancing the body's natural defenses against pathogens. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Silybum marianum teas also have antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat bacterial, viral, and fungal infections. By drinking Silybum marianum teas, individuals may experience improved immune function and a reduced risk of infection.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal teas for infection
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Silybum marianum leaves. You can find them in dry or health stores.
Wash the leaves with clean water to remove dirt and impurities.
Chop 2 tablespoons of the leaves into small pieces.
Steep the chopped leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and let it cool before drinking. Drink 1 cup, 2-3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium teas contains sesquiterpene lactones, triterpenoid saponins, and flavonoids. These compounds have antimicrobial properties that help fight off infections by inhibiting the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi. The sesquiterpene lactones in particular, such as achillol and dihydroachillol, have been shown to disrupt the cell walls of microorganisms, preventing them from multiplying and causing infection. The triterpenoid saponins also contribute to the tea's antimicrobial properties by binding to and disrupting the cell membranes of microorganisms. Additionally, the flavonoids in Achillea millefolium, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the body.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal teas for infection
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Achillea millefolium leaves.
Combine 1 cup of leaves with 2 cups of boiling water in a saucepan.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Add honey to taste, if needed. The recommended dose is 1 cup of tea, 3 times a day.
Drink the tea to help with infections. Consult a doctor before using, especially if pregnant or breastfeeding.
